title: Lakshya Sen Advances to China Open Last 16 After Tough Opening Win
indian_angle: India's top male shuttler Lakshya Sen has started his China Open campaign with a hard-fought victory.
summary: India No. 1 Lakshya Sen defeated Japan's Yushi Tanaka in a three-game thriller, 22-20, 15-21, 21-14, to progress to the Round of 16 at the China Open.

title: Ayush Shetty Triumphs Over Alwi Farhan in China Open Thriller
indian_angle: Young Indian talent Ayush Shetty secured a significant win against a promising opponent at the China Open.
summary: Rising Indian shuttler Ayush Shetty emerged victorious against Malaysia's Alwi Farhan in a closely contested match at the China Open, marking a notable performance for the young star.

title: Devika, Prannoy Suffer Early Exits at China Masters
indian_angle: Indian shuttlers Devika and HS Prannoy faced early defeats in their respective opening rounds at the China Masters.
summary: Thailand Open Super 300 champion Devika lost a tough three-game match to Busanan Ongbamrungphan, while HS Prannoy also exited the China Masters on the opening day.

title: PV Sindhu Reflects on Emotional Japan Open Title Win
indian_angle: PV Sindhu's recent Japan Open victory is a major comeback for India's star shuttler, silencing critics and reaffirming her elite status.
summary: PV Sindhu expressed her emotional relief and joy after winning the Japan Open, a victory she described as answering her doubters and bringing tears to her eyes.

title: Virat Kohli's Remark Inspired PV Sindhu's Recent Comeback
indian_angle: This story reveals the surprising influence of cricket star Virat Kohli on PV Sindhu's return to form, a fascinating insight for Indian sports fans.
summary: A cryptic remark from cricketer Virat Kohli reportedly played a pivotal role in motivating PV Sindhu's resurgence on the badminton court, leading to her recent successes.

title: New 3x15 Scoring System to Debut in Indian Domestic Badminton
indian_angle: This new scoring system will significantly impact how domestic badminton tournaments are played across India, affecting many aspiring players.
summary: The Badminton Association of India (BAI) has implemented a new 3x15 scoring system for all domestic ranking tournaments and national championships (U-13, U-15 & U-17), set to debut at Ernakulam.
